findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Analysis/PDE/UniformEllipticity.lean:180` has the stronger `{c lam : ℝ} (hlam : 0 < lam) (hlamc : lam ≤ c)` signature; Mathlib defines `IsCoercive` as `∃ C, 0 < C ∧ ...` at `mathlib/Mathlib/Analysis/Normed/Operator/NormedSpace.lean:293`, so `0 < c` suffices by taking witness `c`.
findings.fix: State the natural theorem as `lemma isCoercive_matrixBilinearForm_smul_one {c : ℝ} (hc : 0 < c) : IsCoercive (matrixBilinearForm (c • (1 : Matrix n n ℝ)))`; derive any `_of_le` wrapper from it if callers need that spelling.
findings.issue: `isCoercive_matrixBilinearForm_smul_one_of_le` requires `0 < lam` and `lam ≤ c`, but the conclusion is only `IsCoercive ...`, whose witness is existential and does not expose `lam`.

summary: One coercivity helper is stated with an unnecessary explicit lower constant even though `IsCoercive` hides the witness existentially. The scalar ellipticity constructors otherwise sit at the same concrete real Euclidean matrix level as the surrounding PDE API.
